game_name: Déjà Vu: A Nightmare Comes True

design_philosophy.core_loop: Investigate crime scene, gather clues through exploration and dialogue, solve lateral thinking puzzles to progress narrative and uncover truth about Ace's amnesia and the murder frame
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Memory deterioration mechanic creates time pressure (modified on NES to unlimited time before hard death at Ace's office); cognitive fog versus investigative clarity as core mechanical metaphor
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Play as hardboiled 1940s detective clearing your name and recovering lost identity; embody noir detective solving a complex murder mystery through lateral thinking rather than combat
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Rejects combat-heavy adventure game design in favor of puzzle-solving and investigation; point-and-click interface deliberately distances player from text parser frustration; prioritizes literary noir atmosphere over mechanical complexity; memory loss is both narrative device and mechanical constraint

difficulty.checkpointing: No explicit checkpointing mentioned; NES version modified memory deterioration mechanic to allow unlimited time with hard death trigger only at specific location (Ace's office without antidote)
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player must manage exploration pacing to find antidote before memory fully deteriorates (modified on NES); puzzle solutions require lateral thinking with detective-based or violent approaches available; boxing background provides combat option

progression.upgrade_system: Antidote acquisition is primary progression gate; obtaining antidote enables memory flashbacks that provide essential narrative information for solving subsequent puzzles
progression.unlock_mechanism: Location-based progression: player unlocks taxi access to new locations (office, mansion, etc.) by gathering addresses and solving puzzles in prior areas; antidote unlocks cognitive clarity and narrative flashbacks
replayability_hooks: Significant lateral thinking requirements may yield different solution paths on replay
Multiple puzzle-solving approaches (detective techniques vs. violence)
Player knowledge of antidote location changes replay experience

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling: strap-down chair, vials, syringe suggest interrogation and drug use in physical space
Character encounters reveal plot details: mugger, old acquaintances, police create adversarial encounters with narrative weight
Location-based narrative: taxi travel to offices, mansions, bars creates geography of investigation
Amnesia mechanic as narrative-mechanical fusion: progressive memory loss reflects player's cognitive state; flashbacks after antidote restore narrative clarity
Hardboiled noir atmosphere permeates setting (1942 Chicago), dialogue, and aesthetic

faction_indicators: Police pursuing Ace as murder suspect
Old acquaintances with grudges representing past debts
Bar patrons and underworld figures (Joe's Bar setting)
danger_signals: Framing for murder creates legal/social danger
Memory deterioration threatens Ace's cognition and survival
Sternwood Mansion contains deadly trap for amnesiac player
environmental_storytelling: Joe's Bar bathroom stall as noir opening location
Dead body in upstairs office establishes crime
Interrogation evidence (chair, vials, needle mark) suggests forced drugging
Multiple locations tied to kidnapping conspiracy reveal through exploration
